description_long: This is a khaki green backpack with a classic, utilitarian design, featuring a main compartment with a triangular flap and a leather strap closure at the front. The front face displays two decorative patches: one with a cartoon astronaut and another with a mountain and the word 'Oregon.' The bottom and trim of the backpack are reinforced with darker brown leather, providing durability and contrast. The shoulder straps are made of the same khaki fabric, are adjustable, and attach at the top and bottom corners. The backpack appears to be made primarily of canvas with leather accents and has a soft, slightly wrinkled texture. There are no visible side or mesh pockets, and the overall shape is slightly rounded and flexible.
description: A khaki green canvas backpack with brown leather trim, two decorative patches on the front, and adjustable fabric straps.
description_view_0: Side view showing the curved profile, single main compartment, and leather-reinforced bottom.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the triangular flap, leather strap closure, and two decorative patches.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the rounded shape and strap attachment.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the two adjustable shoulder straps and plain back panel.
attribute_brand_logo: There is a patch on the upper front with a cartoon astronaut and a circular background, and a rectangular patch below it with a mountain graphic and the word 'Oregon.' No explicit brand logo is visible.
attribute_strap_design: Two adjustable, non-padded shoulder straps made of khaki canvas, medium width, attached at the top and bottom corners of the back panel.
attribute_zipper_placement: None visible; the main compartment appears to use a flap and leather strap closure.
attribute_pocket_configuration: One main compartment accessed via the front flap; no visible external or side pockets.
attribute_decorative_elements: Two decorative patches on the front: one with a cartoon astronaut and one with a mountain and 'Oregon.'
